tools.description: Store a memory; deduplicates by title+namespace.
tools.docs: Store a memory. Dedupes by (title, namespace). Tier defaults to mid (7d TTL); long is permanent. on_conflict: error|merge|version. scope: Task 1.5 visibility. force (#519): bypass proactive contradiction detection on near-duplicate writes.

on_conflict.description: P2/G6 (title,ns) collision: error=v2 default; merge=v1; version='(N)'.

tools.name: memory_store

tools.description: Recall memories relevant to a context (ranked).
tools.docs: Fuzzy OR recall ranked by relevance + priority + access + tier. Optional: budget_tokens (cl100k cap), context_tokens (query-embed bias), session_id (+0.05 recency boost per #518), session_default (splice [agents.defaults.recall_scope]), include_archived, kinds filter. Default format toon_compact (~79% smaller).

budget_tokens.description: P6/R1 cl100k content cap. 0=empty; top kept (meta.budget_overflow=true).

context_tokens.description: Recent conversation tokens; biases query embedding 70/30 (v0.6.0.0).

format.description: Response format. toon_compact saves 79% vs json.

include_archived.description: WT-1-E: include atomised sources alongside atoms.

kinds.description: Form 6 (#759) kind filter. Array/CSV. OR within; AND across.

session_id.description: #518 session id; +0.05 rerank boost for in-session ring (cap 50).

tools.name: memory_recall

tools.description: List recall_observations (#886).
tools.docs: Gap 3 (#886): recall-consumption ledger filter.
tools.name: memory_recall_observations

tools.description: Search memories by exact keyword match (AND semantics).
tools.docs: Exact keyword AND search. Deterministic; no fuzzy/semantic. Filters: namespace, tier, agent_id, as_agent (Task 1.5 scope). WT-1-E: atomised sources hidden by default.
tools.name: memory_search

tools.description: List memories, optionally filtered by namespace or tier.
tools.docs: Browse memories. Filters: namespace, tier, agent_id. Limit caps at 200.
tools.name: memory_list

tools.description: Load top-k recent + high-priority memories from a Family.
tools.docs: B1: top-k by metadata.family. Always-on; alternative to memory_recall when family is known. Issue #864 — `family` here is the MCP tool family (8 groups: core/lifecycle/graph/governance/power/meta/archive/other), NOT the memory_kind taxonomy (Observation/Reflection/Plan/Decision/etc).

family.description: MCP tool family (8 groups) — NOT the memory_kind taxonomy. See #864.

tools.name: memory_load_family

tools.description: Intent-routed loader: free-text intent picks the best Family.
tools.docs: B2: pick best Family from free-text intent, then forward to memory_load_family. Issue #864 — `Family` here is the MCP tool family (8 groups: core/lifecycle/graph/governance/power/meta/archive/other), NOT the memory_kind taxonomy (Observation/Reflection/Plan/Decision/etc).
tools.name: memory_smart_load

tools.description: Return a hierarchical tree of namespaces with memory counts.
tools.docs: Pillar 1 / Stream A: namespace tree (live rows only). Each node has count + subtree_count. Response includes total_count and truncated flag.

depth.description: Max descent. Deeper rows roll up into boundary subtree_count.

limit.description: Row cap. Densest namespaces win on truncation.

namespace_prefix.description: Restrict to this namespace + descendants. Trailing '/' tolerated.

tools.name: memory_get_taxonomy

tools.description: Pre-write near-duplicate check via cosine over stored embeddings.
tools.docs: Pillar 2 / Stream D: pre-write near-dup check. Embeds title+content, returns highest-cosine match + is_duplicate + suggested_merge. Threshold floor 0.5. Requires semantic tier+.

threshold.description: Cosine threshold; floor 0.5. Default 0.85 tuned for MiniLM-L6-v2.

tools.name: memory_check_duplicate

tools.description: Register an entity (canonical name + aliases) under a namespace.
tools.docs: Pillar 2 / Stream B: register entity as long-tier memory (metadata.kind='entity'). Idempotent on (canonical_name, namespace); merges new aliases. Errors if name collides with a non-entity row.
tools.name: memory_entity_register

tools.description: Resolve an alias to its registered entity.
tools.docs: Pillar 2 / Stream B: resolve alias to entity. Without namespace, most-recently-created wins. Null when no match.
tools.name: memory_entity_get_by_alias

tools.description: Ordered fact timeline for an entity (outbound KG links by valid_from).
tools.docs: Pillar 2 / Stream C: outbound links from source_id ordered valid_from ASC. Includes valid_from/valid_until/observed_by + target title/namespace. NULL valid_from rows excluded. Cross-namespace.

since.description: RFC3339 inclusive lower bound on valid_from.

until.description: RFC3339 inclusive upper bound on valid_from.

tools.name: memory_kg_timeline

tools.description: Mark a KG link as superseded by setting its valid_until column.
tools.docs: Pillar 2 / Stream C: set valid_until on (source_id, target_id, relation). valid_until defaults to now. Idempotent; response carries previous_valid_until. found:false when no match.
tools.name: memory_kg_invalidate

tools.description: Outbound KG traversal from a source memory (<=5 hops).
tools.docs: Pillar 2 / Stream C: BFS/CTE traversal with cycle detection. Each row carries valid_from/valid_until/observed_by + target title/namespace. Filters chain across every hop. max_depth ceiling 5.

allowed_agents.description: Observed-by allowlist. Empty array = zero rows.

valid_at.description: RFC3339; keep links valid at instant. Omit to skip temporal filter.

tools.name: memory_kg_query

tools.description: Enumerate up to N paths through the KG between two memories (BFS, max_depth<=7).
tools.docs: J7: undirected BFS over memory_links with cycle detection. Returns id chains source-first. max_depth<=7, max_results<=50.
tools.name: memory_find_paths

tools.description: Delete a memory by ID.
tools.docs: Hard-delete by id (removes row, embedding, FTS, links). Use memory_forget for bulk pattern delete (archives first).
tools.name: memory_delete

tools.description: Promote a memory to long (or chosen tier) / ancestor namespace.
tools.docs: Default: bump to long (clears expiry); short->long and mid->long are single-call. #831: target_tier ('mid'|'long') stops on intermediate. Task 1.7: to_namespace clones to an ancestor + derived_from link.

target_tier.description: #831: 'mid' keeps expires_at; 'long' clears it. Downgrades rejected.

to_namespace.description: Task 1.7: clone target (must be a proper ancestor).

tools.name: memory_promote

tools.description: Bulk delete memories matching a pattern, namespace, or tier (archives first).
tools.docs: Bulk delete by pattern/namespace/tier. Archives first (recover via memory_archive_restore). dry_run previews.
tools.name: memory_forget

tools.description: Get memory store statistics (counts, tier breakdown, sizes).
tools.docs: Totals, per-tier + namespace tallies, archive + DB size.
tools.name: memory_stats

tools.description: Update an existing memory by ID (only provided fields change).
tools.docs: Partial update by id. Omitted fields preserved. Tier monotone-only. metadata.agent_id preserved.
tools.name: memory_update

tools.description: Get a specific memory by ID, including its links.
tools.docs: Memory row + linked ids (in+out). Use memory_get_links for full link rows with attestation.
tools.name: memory_get

tools.description: Create a typed link between two memories.
tools.docs: Directional link. Relations: related_to | supersedes | contradicts | derived_from | reflects_on (Task 3/8). H-track signs with active Ed25519 (verify via memory_verify).
tools.name: memory_link

tools.description: Get all links for a memory (both directions).
tools.docs: In + outbound links with relation, attest_level (unsigned/self_signed/peer_attested), valid_from/until/observed_by.
tools.name: memory_get_links

tools.description: Re-verify a stored memory_links row's Ed25519 signature on demand.
tools.docs: H4: re-verify link signature. Returns {signature_verified, attest_level, signed_by, signed_at}. Pass link_id composite ('source--relation-->target') or explicit triple.
tools.name: memory_verify

tools.description: Reconstruct the conversation transcript chain that produced a memory.
tools.docs: I4: transcript chain (text + span metadata). verbose=false (default) truncates >100KB entries. L2-4 (#669): for reflections, walks reflects_on edges for transcript UNION; cap via depth (null=full, 0=self only).
tools.name: memory_replay

tools.description: Persist a reflection memory plus reflects_on provenance links to each source.
tools.docs: Task 4/8 (#655): substrate-native recursive-learning primitive. reflection_depth = max(source_depths)+1; gated by namespace governance.max_reflection_depth (Task 2/8) — refusal returns REFLECTION_DEPTH_EXCEEDED. New memory + N reflects_on links land in one atomic txn.

metadata.description: Merged with system reflection_metadata; caller keys win.

namespace.description: Target namespace. Defaults to first source's namespace.

source_ids.description: Sources reflected on; one reflects_on link per id.

tools.name: memory_reflect

tools.description: Render a single reflection memory as markdown or JSON (no filesystem write).
tools.docs: QW-1: render reflection + reflects_on provenance as YAML-frontmatter md (default) or JSON envelope. Returns {content, suggested_filename}. No FS write — harness owns disk I/O.
tools.name: memory_export_reflection

tools.description: Fetch the latest Persona artefact for an entity (read-only).
tools.docs: QW-2: latest MemoryKind::Persona for (entity_id, namespace). Returns envelope {id, entity_id, namespace, body_md, sources, generated_at, version, attest_level}. null when none. Pair with memory_persona_generate.
tools.name: memory_persona

tools.description: Generate/regen a Persona artefact for an entity.
tools.docs: QW-2 / #848: synthesise MemoryKind::Persona from top-K Reflection memories. Omit namespace (or pass null) for cross-namespace aggregation (#848 — persona lands in 'global'); pass a namespace string for single-namespace scope. Response includes namespace_scope=single|cross_namespace.
tools.name: memory_persona_generate

tools.description: Inspect the cross-peer provenance of a reflection memory.
tools.docs: L2-2 (S6-M1): {memory_id, peer_origin, signing_agent, original_depth, local_depth_at_arrival, is_reflection}. Non-reflections return envelope with is_reflection=false. Unknown ids => error.
tools.name: memory_reflection_origin

tools.description: List dependents flagged by the L2-3 invalidation walker.
tools.docs: L2-3 (#668): read-only list of memories with reflects_on->memory_id. Notification, NOT cascade — dependents are flagged for curator review. Returns {memory_id, count, dependents:[{id, namespace}]}. Unknown ids => empty.
tools.name: memory_dependents_of_invalidated

tools.description: Consolidate multiple memories into one long-term summary.
tools.docs: Merge 2-100 sources into one long-tier memory; deletes sources, adds derived_from links. LLM auto-generates summary if omitted (smart/autonomous tier).

summary.description: Optional summary; LLM auto-generates at smart/autonomous tier.

tools.name: memory_consolidate

tools.description: Form 3 multi-step ingest: deterministic helpers + LLM stages.
tools.docs: Form 3 (#756): two_phase (FTS + Jaccard -> synthesise) or four_step (load_context -> classify -> enrich -> emit). Helpers run first; LLM stages receive helper output under explicit-trust banner + SHARED PREFIX for cache-key reuse. Response carries trace + cache-key set + final output. Smart+ tier only.

pipeline_variant.description: Named pipeline; ignored if pipeline_override set.

tools.name: memory_ingest_multistep

tools.description: Decompose a memory into 2-10 atomic propositions; source archived. Smart+ tier.
tools.docs: WT-1-C: atomise via WT-1-B engine. Atoms = Observation memories with metadata.atom_source_id + derives_from link. Source archived (atomised_into=N). Returns {source_id, atom_ids, atom_count, archived_at}. Idempotent (use force_re_atomise to mint fresh). Too-small sources => {source_too_small:true}. Failures => CURATOR_FAILED / GOVERNANCE_REFUSED envelopes.

force_re_atomise.description: Skip idempotency; mint fresh atoms (old retained).

tools.name: memory_atomise

tools.description: Share a memory with another agent (copy into _shared/<from>→<to>/).
tools.docs: #224/#311 MVP: point-to-point copy into `_shared/<from>→<to>/` with provenance.

source_memory_id.description: Memory id (full UUID or unique prefix) to share.

target_agent_id.description: Recipient agent id; must satisfy validate_agent_id.

tools.name: memory_share

tools.description: Scan confidence_shadow_observations and emit per-source baselines (Form 5).
tools.docs: Form 5 (#758): read-only calibration sweep over shadow-mode observations (AI_MEMORY_CONFIDENCE_SHADOW=1). Returns CalibrationReport {window_days, total_observations, baselines:[{namespace, source, count, median, mean, buckets}]}. Default window 30d. Family::Power — refuses on keyword tier.
tools.name: memory_calibrate_confidence

tools.description: Discover runtime capabilities; family=<name> drills in.
tools.docs: Caps-v3: tier, profile, summary, callable_now, agent_permitted_families, harness detection. family+include_schema drills one family. verbose=true restores full schema. NOTE per #864: `family` here = MCP tool-family (8 groups: core/lifecycle/graph/governance/power/meta/archive/other), NOT memory_kind taxonomy.

accept.description: Schema version. v2 default; v1 legacy.

include_schema.description: Return full tool schemas. Requires family.

verbose.description: C2/C4: preserve docs + every optional inputSchema property.

tools.name: memory_capabilities

tools.description: LLM-expand a search query into related terms (smart/autonomous tier).
tools.docs: LLM query expansion. Smart/autonomous tier.
tools.name: memory_expand_query

tools.description: LLM-generate tags for a memory (smart/autonomous tier).
tools.docs: LLM auto-tagging. Smart/autonomous tier.
tools.name: memory_auto_tag

tools.description: LLM-check whether two memories contradict each other (smart/autonomous tier).
tools.docs: LLM contradiction check. Smart/autonomous tier.
tools.name: memory_detect_contradiction

tools.description: List archived (expired) memories.
tools.docs: List archived memories. Filter by namespace; paginate via offset/limit.
tools.name: memory_archive_list

tools.description: Restore an archived memory back to the active store.
tools.docs: Restore archived row; expires_at cleared.
tools.name: memory_archive_restore

tools.description: Permanently delete archived memories.
tools.docs: Purge archive. Scope via older_than_days. Unrecoverable.

older_than_days.description: Only purge entries older than N days.

tools.name: memory_archive_purge

tools.description: Show archive statistics (total count and per-namespace breakdown).
tools.docs: Archive total + per-namespace counts.
tools.name: memory_archive_stats

tools.description: Trigger garbage collection on expired memories (archives first).
tools.docs: GC expired memories. Archives first when archive_on_gc is on (default). dry_run previews.
tools.name: memory_gc

tools.description: Auto-recall recent memories on session start.
tools.docs: Most-recently-accessed/updated. At smart/autonomous tier, includes LLM summary.
tools.name: memory_session_start

tools.description: Set a memory as the standard/policy for a namespace.
tools.docs: Standard memory auto-prepended to recall + session_start. Rule layering: global '*' + parent chain + namespace. Task 1.8: governance policy merged into metadata. P4/G1: inherit flag.
tools.name: memory_namespace_set_standard

tools.description: Get the standard/policy memory for a namespace.
tools.docs: Returns the standard. inherit=true (Task 1.6) returns the resolved chain (global '*' -> ancestors -> namespace).
tools.name: memory_namespace_get_standard

tools.description: Clear the standard/policy for a namespace.
tools.docs: Clear the namespace standard.
tools.name: memory_namespace_clear_standard

tools.description: List pending governance-queued actions.
tools.docs: Task 1.9: list governance-queued actions. status filter (default pending). Limit cap 1000.
tools.name: memory_pending_list

tools.description: Approve a pending action; `remember` auto-decides next time.
tools.docs: Task 1.9 approve. decided_by = caller. K10: remember (once|session|forever) writes a synthetic permit rule.
tools.name: memory_pending_approve

tools.description: Reject a pending action; `remember` auto-decides next time.
tools.docs: Task 1.9 reject. decided_by = caller. K10: remember writes a synthetic deny rule.
tools.name: memory_pending_reject

tools.description: Register an agent in the reserved _agents namespace.
tools.docs: Register agent (agent_type, capabilities) in _agents. Refreshes last_seen_at; preserves registered_at. agent_id is CLAIMED, not attested — pair with attestation for security boundary.

agent_type.description: Curated: human, system, ai:<model>. Open-form: any ai:<name>.

tools.name: memory_agent_register

tools.description: List every registered agent.
tools.docs: List agents (ordered by registered_at).
tools.name: memory_agent_list

tools.description: Send a message from the caller to another agent's inbox.
tools.docs: Send message to _messages/<target>. Sender = caller agent_id. Read via memory_inbox.
tools.name: memory_notify

tools.description: List messages sent to an agent via memory_notify.
tools.docs: Read _messages/<agent_id>. access_count==0 is the unread marker.
tools.name: memory_inbox

tools.description: Register a webhook subscription for memory events.
tools.docs: Webhook subscription. HMAC-SHA256 signed via X-Ai-Memory-Signature when secret supplied. https required (http only for loopback). Secret stored hashed only.

events.description: Comma-list or *. Events: memory_store, memory_delete, memory_promote.

url.description: https URL (http only for loopback). SSRF guard rejects private IPs.

tools.name: memory_subscribe

tools.description: Delete a subscription by id.
tools.docs: Delete subscription. DLQ rows retained for audit.
tools.name: memory_unsubscribe

tools.description: List active webhook subscriptions.
tools.docs: List subscriptions. Secrets never returned.
tools.name: memory_list_subscriptions

tools.description: Replay subscription_events since an RFC3339 timestamp.
tools.docs: K7: replay events ordered by delivered_at asc.
tools.name: memory_subscription_replay

tools.description: List subscription_dlq rows (exhausted retry ladder).
tools.docs: K7: DLQ inspector.
tools.name: memory_subscription_dlq_list

tools.description: Report per-agent + per-namespace quota usage. Operator-facing.
tools.docs: K8/#1156: per-agent + per-namespace quota usage (memories/day, storage bytes, links/day). Omit agent_id for all. Omit namespace for the aggregate view (sum across namespaces). Supply both for the single row.

namespace.description: Restrict to one namespace (v0.7.0 #1156 — per-namespace K8 dimension). When supplied with `agent_id`, returns the single (agent_id, namespace) row. When supplied without `agent_id`, returns every agent's row in that namespace. When omitted, the aggregate view is returned for an agent_id or the full substrate listing otherwise.

tools.name: memory_quota_status

tools.description: Check action vs governance_rules (#691); Allow/Refuse/Warn.
tools.docs: #691: read-only rule check. Harness PreToolUse hook calls on every Bash/Write/Edit. Rule MUTATION over MCP is disabled — use `ai-memory rules --sign` CLI or signed HTTP admin endpoints.
tools.name: memory_check_agent_action

tools.description: List substrate-level agent-action rules. Read-only (#691).
tools.docs: #691: governance_rules read. Mutation operator-only (CLI/HTTP signed); MCP read-only by design 2026-05-13.
tools.name: memory_rule_list

tools.description: Register an agentskills.io SKILL.md from a folder or inline text.
tools.docs: L1-5: Ed25519-attested skill registration with version chaining. Re-register same (name, namespace) supersedes prior row.
tools.name: memory_skill_register

tools.description: List current (non-superseded) skills; body not returned.
tools.docs: L1-5: discovery (name, description, id, namespace, digest, metadata). Use memory_skill_get for body.
tools.name: memory_skill_list

tools.description: Get full skill activation payload (metadata + body).
tools.docs: L1-5: metadata + decompressed body (<5000 tok). Old version ids stay addressable.
tools.name: memory_skill_get

tools.description: Fetch + digest-verify a skill resource.
tools.docs: L1-5: SHA-256-verified resource fetch. Errors on mismatch.
tools.name: memory_skill_resource

tools.description: Export a skill to a folder; re-register produces identical digest.
tools.docs: L1-5: write SKILL.md + resources/ to target_folder. Round-trip identical SHA-256. Emits skill.exported signed_events row.
tools.name: memory_skill_export

tools.description: Promote a Reflection into a reusable Agent Skill.
tools.docs: L2-6 (#671): reflection (depth>=namespace.governance.skill_promotion_min_depth, default 1) -> SKILL.md. Each reflects_on source -> references/source_{i}.md. Frontmatter preserves derived_from_reflection_id + original_reflection_depth. Promote->export->register => identical SHA-256. Refuses depth-0.

parameters_schema.description: Optional JSON schema spliced as Parameters section.

tools.name: memory_skill_promote_from_reflection

tools.description: Skill body + composes_with_reflections (bounded by max_reflection_depth).
tools.docs: L2-7 (#672): compose skill activation with reflections from SKILL.md composes_with_reflections list. Per-entry min_depth filter; per-namespace max_reflection_depth is the authoritative ceiling (CANNOT bypass bounded-recursion). Reflections ranked recency + recall_count; budget_tokens caps cumulative reflection content (default 4000, max 32000).

budget_tokens.description: cl100k cap on reflection content. Default 4000, max 32000.

tools.name: memory_skill_compositional_context

tools.description: Offload verbatim content; returns ref_id (Family::Power).
tools.docs: QW-3 follow-up: store verbatim in offloaded_blobs. Returns {ref_id, content_sha256, stored_at}. Dereference via memory_deref. Semantic+ tier.
tools.name: memory_offload

tools.description: Dereference a memory_offload ref_id (Family::Power).
tools.docs: QW-3 follow-up: sha256-verified lookup. Returns {ref_id, content, stored_at, sha256}. Refuses tampered rows. Semantic+ tier.
tools.name: memory_deref
